query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: 2c163c68).

## Issue to Resolve

Change default code style to `ktlint_official`
Starting of ktlint `1.0` the default code style changes from `intellij_idea` (former `kotlin_official`) to `ktlint_official`. The  `ktlint_official` codestyle  adheres to the best parts of  the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android Kotlint styleguid](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). But it no longer is restricted to produce code compatible with the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter which  lacks from bug which still are not resolved after multiple years.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/editorconfig/CodeStyleEditorConfigProperty.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ClassSignatureRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.EnumValueParser
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
 8→ */
 9→@Suppress("EnumEntryName")
10→public enum class CodeStyleValue {
11→    /**
12→     * Code formatting based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This
13→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in Android Studio.
14→     */
15→    android_studio,
16→
17→    /**
18→     * Code formatting based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). This
19→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in IntelliJ IDEA.
20→     */
21→    intellij_idea,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
25→     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
26→     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
27→     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
28→     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
29→     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
30→     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
31→     * are still not fixed.
32→     * In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
33→     */
34→    ktlint_official,
35→}
36→
37→public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE: PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType<CodeStyleValue> =
38→    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
39→        "ktlint_code_style",
40→        "The code style ('intellij_idea', 'android_studio' or 'ktlint_official') to be applied. Currently, the " +
41→            "value is defaulted to 'intellij_idea' when not set. However, in the future the default code style " +
42→            "will be changed to 'ktlint_official'.",
43→        SafeEnumValueParser(CodeStyleValue::class.java),
44→        CodeStyleValue.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
45→    )
46→
47→public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<CodeStyleValue> =
48→    EditorConfigProperty(
49→        type = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE,
50→        /*
51→         * Once the [CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official] is matured, it will become the default code style of ktlint. Until
52→         * then the [CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea] is used to remain backwards compatible.
53→         */
54→        defaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
55→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.android_studio,
56→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
57→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
58→    )
59→

CHANGELOG.md:174:Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
--
CHANGELOG.md:180:A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
--
CHANGELOG.md:182:This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
--
CHANGELOG.md:186:The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
--
CHANGELOG.md:188:Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
--
CHANGELOG.md:367:Previously, the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead.
--
CHANGELOG.md:381:* Add new code style `ktlint_offical`. The code style is work in progress and should be considered a preview. It is intended to become the default code style in the next release. Please try it out and give your feedback. See [code styles](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/code-styles/) for more information. The following rules have been added to the `ktlint_official` code style (the rules can also be run for other code styles when enabled explicitly):
--
CHANGELOG.md:639:* The default value for trailing commas on call site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on call site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1670))
CHANGELOG.md:640:* The default value for trailing commas on declaration site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on declaration site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1669](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1669))
--
CHANGELOG.md:932:This property defaults to the `official` Kotlin code style when not set.

 49→/**
 50→ * Formats the class signature according to https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#class-headers
 51→ *
 52→ * In code style `ktlint_official` class headers containing 2 or more parameters are formatted as multiline signature. As the Kotlin Coding
 53→ * conventions do not specify what is meant with a "few parameters", no default is set for other code styles.
 54→ */
 55→public class ClassSignatureRule :
 56→    StandardRule(
 57→        id = "class-signature",
 58→        visitorModifiers =
 59→            setOf(
 60→                // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 61→                RunAsLateAsPossible,
 62→                RunAfterRule(DISCOURAGED_COMMENT_LOCATION_RULE_ID, ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED),
 63→            ),
 64→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 65→            setOf(
 66→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 67→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 68→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 69→                FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 70→            ),
 71→    ),
 72→    Rule.Experimental {
 73→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 74→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 75→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 76→    private var classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 77→
 78→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 79→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 80→        classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY]
 81→        indentConfig =
 82→            IndentConfig(
 83→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 84→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 85→            )
 86→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 90→        node: ASTNode,
 91→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 92→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 93→    ) {
 94→        if (node.elementType == CLASS) {
 95→            visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 96→        }
 97→    }
 98→
 99→    private fun ASTNode.classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean): List<ASTNode> {
100→        // Find the nodes that are to be placed on the same line if no max line length is set
101→        //     internal class Foo(bar: String) { // Class without super type
102→        // or
103→        //     public class Foo(bar: String) : Bar { // Class with exactly one super type
104→        // or
105→        //     private class Foo(bar: String) : // Class with multiple super types which have to be wrapped to next lines
106→        val firstCodeChild = getFirstChildInSignature()
107→        val lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine =
108→            takeIf { excludeSuperTypes }
109→                // When the class extends multiple super types or if the super type list contains a newline, all super types are wrapped
110→                // on separate line
111→                ?.findChildByType(COLON)
112→                ?: findChildByType(CLASS_BODY)?.firstChildNode
113→        return collectLeavesRecursively()
114→            .childrenBetween(
115→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
116→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine },
117→            )
118→    }
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.superTypes() =
121→        findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
122→            ?.children()
123→            ?.filterNot { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() || it.elementType == COMMA }
124→
125→    private fun ASTNode.hasMultilineSuperTypeList() = findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)?.textContains('\n') == true
126→
127→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstChildInSignature(): ASTNode? {
128→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
129→            ?.let { modifierList ->
130→                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
131→                var currentNode: ASTNode
132→                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
133→                    currentNode = iterator.next()
134→                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
135→                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
136→                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
137→                    ) {
138→                        return currentNode
139→                    }
140→                }
141→                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
142→            }
143→        return nextCodeLeaf()
144→    }
145→
146→    private fun visitClass(
147→        node: ASTNode,
148→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150→    ) {
151→        require(node.elementType == CLASS)
152→
153→        val wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters =
154→            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
155→                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
156→                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter()) ||
157→                (isMaxLineLengthSet() && classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(node, emit, autoCorrect)) ||
158→                (!isMaxLineLengthSet() && node.classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesIsMultiline())
159→        fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters, dryRun = false)
160→        fixWhitespacesInSuperTypeList(node, emit, autoCorrect, wrappedPrimaryConstructor = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters)
161→        fixClassBody(node, emit, autoCorrect)
162→    }
163→
164→    private fun classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(
165→        node: ASTNode,
166→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168→    ): Boolean {
169→        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = true)
170→        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, excluding the super types, would be rewritten as single
171→        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
172→        // actual fix.
173→        val length =
174→            actualClassSignatureLength +
175→                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
176→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
177→        return length > maxLineLength
178→    }
179→
180→    private fun ASTNode.classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesIsMultiline() =
181→        classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes = true)
182→            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
183→
184→    private fun ASTNode.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean) =
185→        indent(false).length + getClassSignatureNodesLength(excludeSuperTypes)
186→
187→    private fun ASTNode.getClassSignatureNodesLength(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean) =
188→        classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes)
189→            .joinTextToString()
190→            .length
191→
192→    private fun ASTNode.containsMultilineParameter(): Boolean =
193→        getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
194→            ?.children()
195→            .orEmpty()
196→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
197→            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
198→
199→    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean =
200→        getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
201→            ?.children()
202→            .orEmpty()
203→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
204→            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
205→
206→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
207→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
208→            ?.children()
209→            .orEmpty()
210→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
211→
212→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
213→        node: ASTNode,
214→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
215→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
216→        multiline: Boolean,
217→        dryRun: Boolean,
218→    ): Int {
219→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
220→
221→        val primaryConstructorParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
222→        val hasNoValueParameters =
223→            primaryConstructorParameterList
224→                ?.children()
225→                .orEmpty()
226→                .none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
227→
228→        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
229→            if (hasNoValueParameters) {
230→                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
231→            } else {
232→                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
233→                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
234→                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
235→            }
236→
237→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
238→    }
239→
240→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
241→        node: ASTNode,
242→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
243→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
244→        dryRun: Boolean,
245→    ): Int {
246→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
247→
248→        node
249→            .getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
250→            ?.takeUnless { it.containsComment() }
251→            ?.let { parameterList ->
252→                if (!dryRun) {
253→                    emit(parameterList.startOffset, "No parenthesis expected", true)
254→                }
255→                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
256→                    parameterList.treeParent.removeChild(parameterList)
257→                } else {
258→                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= parameterList.textLength
259→                }
260→            }
261→
262→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
263→    }
264→
265→    private fun ASTNode.containsComment() = children().any { it.isPartOfComment() }
266→
267→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
268→        node: ASTNode,
269→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
270→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
271→        multiline: Boolean,
272→        dryRun: Boolean,
273→    ): Int {
274→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
275→
276→        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
277→        val firstParameterInList =
278→            valueParameterList
279→                ?.children()
280→                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
281→                ?: return 0
282→
283→        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
284→        firstParameter
285→            ?.prevLeaf()
286→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
287→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
288→                if (multiline) {
289→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
290→                        !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
291→                    ) {
292→                        if (!dryRun) {
293→                            emit(firstParameterInList.startOffset, "Newline expected after opening parenthesis", true)
294→                        }
295→                        // Let indent rule determine the exact indent
296→                        val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
297→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
298→                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
299→                        } else {
300→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
301→                        }
302→                    }
303→                } else {
304→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
305→                        if (!dryRun) {
306→                            emit(
307→                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
308→                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
309→                                true,
310→                            )
311→                        }
312→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
313→                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
314→                        } else {
315→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
316→                        }
317→                    }
318→                }
319→            }
320→
321→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
322→    }
323→
324→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
325→        node: ASTNode,
326→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
327→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
328→        multiline: Boolean,
329→        dryRun: Boolean,
330→    ): Int {
331→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
332→
333→        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
334→        val firstParameterInList =
335→            valueParameterList
336→                ?.children()
337→                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
338→                ?: return 0
339→
340→        valueParameterList
341→            .children()
342→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
343→            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
344→            .forEach { valueParameter ->
345→                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
346→                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
347→                    ?.prevLeaf()
348→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
349→                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
350→                        if (multiline) {
351→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
352→                                !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
353→                            ) {
354→                                if (!dryRun) {
355→                                    emit(valueParameter.startOffset, "Parameter should start on a newline", true)
356→                                }
357→                                // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
358→                                val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
359→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
360→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
361→                                } else {
362→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
363→                                }
364→                            }
365→                        } else {
366→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
367→                                if (!dryRun) {
368→                                    emit(firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset, "Single whitespace expected before parameter", true)
369→                                }
370→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
371→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
372→                                } else {
373→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
374→                                }
375→                            }
376→                        }
377→                    }
378→            }
379→
380→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
381→    }
382→
383→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
384→        node: ASTNode,
385→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
386→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
387→        multiline: Boolean,
388→        dryRun: Boolean,
389→    ): Int {
390→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
391→
392→        val closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor =
393→            node
394→                .getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
395→                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
396→        closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor
397→            ?.prevSibling()
398→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
399→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
400→                if (multiline) {
401→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
402→                        !whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textContains('\n')
403→                    ) {
404→                        if (!dryRun) {
405→                            emit(closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor!!.startOffset, "Newline expected before closing parenthesis", true)
406→                        }
407→                        // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
408→                        val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent()
409→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
410→                            closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
411→                        } else {
412→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
413→                        }
414→                    }
415→                } else {
416→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
417→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
418→                    ) {
419→                        if (!dryRun) {
420→                            emit(
421→                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
422→                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
423→                                true,
424→                            )
425→                        }
426→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
427→                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
428→                        } else {
429→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
430→                        }
431→                    }
432→                }
433→            }
434→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
435→    }

437→    private fun fixWhitespacesInSuperTypeList(
438→        node: ASTNode,
439→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
440→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
441→        wrappedPrimaryConstructor: Boolean,
442→    ): Int {
443→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
444→
445→        val superTypes = node.superTypes() ?: return 0
446→
447→        if (superTypes.first().elementType != SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY) {
448→            superTypes
449→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY }
450→                ?.let { superTypeCallEntry ->
451→                    emit(superTypeCallEntry.startOffset, "Super type call must be first super type", true)
452→                    if (autoCorrect) {
453→                        val superTypeList = node.findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST) ?: return 0
454→                        val originalFirstSuperType = superTypes.first()
455→                        val commaBeforeSuperTypeCall = requireNotNull(superTypeCallEntry.prevSibling { it.elementType == COMMA })
456→
457→                        // Remove the whitespace before the super type call and do not insert a new whitespace as it will be fixed later
458→                        superTypeCallEntry
459→                            .prevSibling()
460→                            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
461→                            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeSuperTypeCallEntry ->
462→                                superTypeList.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeSuperTypeCallEntry)
463→                            }
464→
465→                        superTypeList.addChild(superTypeCallEntry, superTypes.first())
466→                        superTypeList.addChild(commaBeforeSuperTypeCall, originalFirstSuperType)
467→                    }
468→                }
469→        }
470→
471→        if (superTypes.count() == 1) {
472→            if (wrappedPrimaryConstructor) {
473→                // Format
474→                //     class ClassWithPrimaryConstructorWhichWillBeWrapped(...) :
475→                //         SomeSuperTypeEntry
476→                // to
477→                //     class ClassWithPrimaryConstructorWhichWillBeWrapped(
478→                //         ...
479→                //     ) : SomeSuperTypeEntry
480→                superTypes
481→                    .first()
482→                    .let { firstSuperType ->
483→                        firstSuperType
484→                            .prevLeaf()
485→                            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
486→                            ?.let { whiteSpaceBeforeSuperType ->
487→                                val expectedWhitespace = " "
488→                                if (whiteSpaceBeforeSuperType.text != expectedWhitespace) {
489→                                    emit(firstSuperType.startOffset, "Expected single space before the super type", true)
490→                                    if (autoCorrect) {
491→                                        firstSuperType.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedWhitespace)
492→                                    }
493→                                }
494→                            }
495→                    }
496→            } else {
497→                superTypes
498→                    .first()
499→                    .firstChildNode
500→                    ?.let { superTypeFirstChildNode ->
501→                        superTypeFirstChildNode
502→                            .prevLeaf()
503→                            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
504→                            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
505→                                if (node.hasMultilineSuperTypeList() ||
506→                                    classSignaturesIncludingFirstSuperTypeExceedsMaxLineLength(node, emit, autoCorrect)
507→                                ) {
508→                                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
509→                                        !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
510→                                    ) {
511→                                        emit(superTypeFirstChildNode.startOffset, "Super type should start on a newline", true)
512→                                        if (autoCorrect) {
513→                                            // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
514→                                            superTypeFirstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
515→                                        }
516→                                    }
517→                                } else {
518→                                    val expectedWhitespace = " "
519→                                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
520→                                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedWhitespace
521→                                    ) {
522→                                        emit(superTypeFirstChildNode.startOffset, "Expected single space before the super type", true)
523→                                        if (autoCorrect) {
524→                                            superTypeFirstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedWhitespace)
525→                                        }
526→                                    }
527→                                }
528→                            }
529→                    }
530→            }
531→        } else {
532→            superTypes
533→                .forEachIndexed { index, superType ->
534→                    val firstChildNodeInSuperType = superType.firstChildNode
535→                    firstChildNodeInSuperType
536→                        ?.prevLeaf()
537→                        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
538→                        .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
539→                            if (index == 0 && node.hasMultilinePrimaryConstructor()) {
540→                                val expectedWhitespace = " "
541→                                if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
542→                                    whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedWhitespace
543→                                ) {
544→                                    emit(firstChildNodeInSuperType.startOffset, "Expected single space before the first super type", true)
545→                                    if (autoCorrect) {
546→                                        firstChildNodeInSuperType.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedWhitespace)
547→                                    }
548→                                }
549→                            } else {
550→                                if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
551→                                    !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
552→                                ) {
553→                                    emit(firstChildNodeInSuperType.startOffset, "Super type should start on a newline", true)
554→                                    if (autoCorrect) {
555→                                        // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
556→                                        firstChildNodeInSuperType.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
557→                                    }
558→                                }
559→                            }
560→                        }
561→                }
562→        }
563→
564→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
565→    }
566→
567→    private fun classSignaturesIncludingFirstSuperTypeExceedsMaxLineLength(
568→        node: ASTNode,
569→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
570→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
571→    ): Boolean {
572→        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = false)
573→        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, including the super types, would be rewritten as single
574→        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
575→        // actual fix.
576→        val length =
577→            actualClassSignatureLength +
578→                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
579→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
580→        return length > maxLineLength
581→    }
582→
583→    private fun ASTNode.hasMultilinePrimaryConstructor() =
584→        findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
585→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
586→            ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
587→            ?.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() }
588→            .isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
589→
590→    private fun fixClassBody(
591→        node: ASTNode,
592→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
593→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
594→    ) {
595→        node
596→            .findChildByType(CLASS_BODY)
597→            ?.let { classBody ->
598→                if (classBody.prevLeaf()?.text != " ") {
599→                    emit(classBody.startOffset, "Expected a single space before class body", true)
600→                    if (autoCorrect) {
601→                        classBody
602→                            .prevLeaf(true)
603→                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
604→                    }
605→                }
606→            }
607→    }
608→
609→    private fun isMaxLineLengthSet() = maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
610→
611→    private fun List<ASTNode>.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> = flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
612→
613→    private fun ASTNode.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> =
614→        if (psi is LeafElement) {
615→            listOf(this)
616→        } else {
617→            children()
618→                .flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
619→                .toList()
620→        }
621→
622→    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
623→        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
624→        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
625→    ): List<ASTNode> {
626→        val iterator = iterator()
627→        var currentNode: ASTNode
628→        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
629→
630→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
631→            currentNode = iterator.next()
632→            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
633→                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
634→                break
635→            }
636→        }
637→
638→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
639→            currentNode = iterator.next()
640→            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
641→            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
642→                break
643→            }
644→        }
645→
646→        return childrenBetween
647→    }
648→
649→    private fun List<ASTNode>.joinTextToString(): String = collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
650→
651→    private fun ASTNode.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters(): Boolean = countParameters() >= classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters
652→
653→    private fun ASTNode.countParameters() =
654→        getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
655→            ?.children()
656→            .orEmpty()
657→            .count { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
658→
659→    private fun ASTNode.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull() =
660→        findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
661→
662→    public companion object {
663→        private const val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET = Int.MAX_VALUE
664→        public val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
665→            EditorConfigProperty(
666→                type =
667→                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
668→                        "ktlint_class_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than",
669→                        "Force wrapping the parameters of the class signature in case it contains at least the specified " +
670→                            "number of parameters even in case the entire Class signature would fit on a single line. " +
671→                            "By default this parameter is not enabled.",
672→                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.POSITIVE_INT_VALUE_PARSER,
673→                        setOf("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8", "9", "unset"),
674→                    ),
675→                defaultValue = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET,
676→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = 1,
677→                propertyMapper = { property, _ ->
678→                    if (property?.isUnset == true) {
679→                        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET
680→                    } else {
681→                        property?.getValueAs<Int>()
682→                    }
683→                },
684→                propertyWriter = { property ->
685→                    if (property == FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET) {
686→                        "unset"
687→                    } else {
688→                        property.toString()
689→                    }
690→                },
691→            )
692→    }
693→}
694→
695→public val CLASS_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID: RuleId = ClassSignatureRule().ruleId
696→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Definition of '.editorconfig' property enriched with fields required by the [com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine].
 8→ *
 9→ * Properties can be:
10→ * - universal `.editorconfig` properties defined
11→ *   [here](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/wiki/EditorConfig-Properties#current-universal-properties)
12→ * - universal IntelliJ IDEA properties defined
13→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-api/src/com/intellij/psi/codeStyle/CommonCodeStyleSettings.java)
14→ * - Kotlin specific properties defined
15→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/master/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java)
16→ */
17→public data class EditorConfigProperty<T>(
18→    /**
19→     * Type of property. Could be one of default ones (see [PropertyType.STANDARD_TYPES]) or custom one.
20→     */
21→    public val type: PropertyType<T>,
22→    /**
23→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties. This default applies to all code styles
24→     * unless the code style specific default value is set.
25→     */
26→    public val defaultValue: T,
27→    /**
28→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'ktlint_official'. When not
29→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
30→     */
31→    public val ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
32→    /**
33→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'intellij_idea'. When not
34→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
35→     */
36→    public val intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
37→    /**
38→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'android_studio'. When not
39→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
40→     */
41→    public val androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
42→    /**
43→     * If set, it maps the actual value set for the property, to another valid value for that property. See example
44→     * below where
45→     * ```kotlin
46→     * propertyMapper = { property, isAndroidCodeStyle ->
47→     *     when {
48→     *         property == null ->
49→     *             // property is not defined in ".editorconfig" file
50→     *         property.isUnset ->
51→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with special value "unset"
52→     *         property.sourceValue == "some-string-value" ->
53→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with a value that needs to be remapped to another
54→     *             // valid value. For example the "max_line_length" property accepts value "off" but is remapped to
55→     *             // "-1" in ktlint.
56→     *        else ->
57→     *             property.getValueAs() // or null
58→     *     }
59→     * }
60→     * ```
61→     * In case the lambda returns a null value then, the code style specific default value or the generic [defaultValue]
62→     * is used.
63→     */
64→    public val propertyMapper: ((Property?, CodeStyleValue) -> T?)? = null,
65→    /**
66→     * Custom function that represents [T] as String. Defaults to the standard `toString()` call. Override the
67→     * default implementation in case you need a different behavior than the standard `toString()` (e.g. for
68→     * collections joinToString() is more applicable).
69→     */
70→    public val propertyWriter: (T) -> String = { it.toString() },
71→    /**
72→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
73→     */
74→    val deprecationWarning: String? = null,
75→    /**
76→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
77→     */
78→    val deprecationError: String? = null,
79→    /**
80→     * Name of the property. A property must be named in case multiple properties are defined for the same type.
81→     * Defaults to the name of the type when not set.
82→     */
83→    public val name: String = type.name,
84→)

conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
 10→
 11→### Removed
 12→
 13→### Fixed
 14→
 15→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 16→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 17→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
 18→* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
 19→* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
 20→* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
 21→* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
 22→* Do not wrap a binary expression after an elvis operator in case the max line length is exceeded ([#2128](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2128))
 23→* Fix indent of IS_EXPRESSION, PREFIX_EXPRESSION and POSTFIX_EXPRESSION in case it contains a linebreak `indent` [#2094](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2094)
 24→* Add new experimental rule `function-literal`. This rule enforces the parameter list of a function literal to be formatted consistently. `function-literal` [#2121](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2121)
 25→
 26→### Changed
 27→
 28→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 29→* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 30→* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 31→* As a part of public API stabilization, configure `binary-compatibility-validator` plugin for compile-time verification of binary compatibility with previous `ktlint` versions ([#2131](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2131))
 32→
 33→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 34→
 35→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 36→
 37→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 38→
 39→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 40→
 41→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 42→
 43→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 44→
 45→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 46→
 47→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 48→
 49→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 50→
 51→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 52→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 53→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 54→
 55→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 56→
 57→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 58→
 59→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 60→
 61→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 62→
 63→### Added
 64→
 65→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 66→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 67→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 68→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 69→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 70→* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 71→* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 72→* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 73→
 74→### Removed
 75→
 76→* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 77→
 78→### Fixed
 79→
 80→* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 81→* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 82→* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 83→* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 84→* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 85→* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 86→
 87→### Changed
 88→
 89→* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 90→* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 91→* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 92→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 93→* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 94→* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 95→* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 96→
 97→## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 98→
 99→### Added
100→
101→### Removed
102→
103→### Fixed
104→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
105→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
106→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
107→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
108→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
109→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
110→* Fix indentation of multiline parameter list in function literal `indent` ([#1976](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1976))
111→* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
112→* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
113→* Clean-up unwanted logging dependencies ([#1998](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1998))
114→* Fix directory traversal for patterns referring to paths outside of current working directory or any of it child directories ([#2002](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2002))
115→* Prevent multiple expressions on same line separated by semicolon ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
116→
117→### Changed
118→
119→* Moved class `Baseline` from `ktlint-cli` to `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline` so that Baseline functionality is reusable for API Consumers.
120→
121→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
122→
123→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
124→
125→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
126→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
127→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
128→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
129→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
130→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
131→
132→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
133→
134→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
135→
136→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
137→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
138→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
139→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
140→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
141→
142→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
143→
144→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
145→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
146→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
147→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
148→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
149→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
150→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
151→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
152→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
153→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
154→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
155→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
156→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
157→
158→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
159→
160→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
161→
162→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
163→
164→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
165→
166→#### Experimental rules
167→
168→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
169→
170→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
171→
172→#### EditorConfig
173→
174→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
175→
176→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
177→
178→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
179→
180→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
181→
182→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
183→
184→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
185→
186→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
187→
188→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
189→
190→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
191→
192→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
193→
194→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
195→
196→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
197→
198→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
199→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
200→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |

{"file_path": "/project/CHANGELOG.md", "old_string": "## Unreleased

### Added

* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)

### Fixed", "new_string": "## Unreleased

### Added

* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)

### Changed

* Change default code style to `ktlint_official`. Previously the default code style was `intellij_idea` (formerly known as `official`). The new `ktlint_official` code style adheres to the best parts of the Kotlin Coding Conventions and Android Kotlin styleguide. However, it is no longer restricted to produce code compatible with the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter which lacks fixes for bugs that still are not resolved after multiple years.

### Removed

### Fixed"}

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→### Added
 8→
 9→* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
10→
11→### Removed
12→
13→### Fixed
14→
15→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
16→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
17→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
18→* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
19→* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
20→* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
21→* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
22→* Do not wrap a binary expression after an elvis operator in case the max line length is exceeded ([#2128](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2128))
23→* Fix indent of IS_EXPRESSION, PREFIX_EXPRESSION and POSTFIX_EXPRESSION in case it contains a linebreak `indent` [#2094](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2094)
24→* Add new experimental rule `function-literal`. This rule enforces the parameter list of a function literal to be formatted consistently. `function-literal` [#2121](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2121)
25→
26→### Changed
27→
28→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
29→* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
30→* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
31→* As a part of public API stabilization, configure `binary-compatibility-validator` plugin for compile-time verification of binary compatibility with previous `ktlint` versions ([#2131](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2131))
32→
33→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
34→
35→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
36→
37→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
38→
39→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
40→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 2→
 3→```ini
 4→[*.{kt,kts}]
 5→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 6→```
 7→
 8→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 9→
10→!!! note
11→    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
12→
13→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
14→
15→* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Be aware that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
16→
17→* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
18→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
 2→
 3→!!! danger
 4→
 5→    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
 6→
 7→## Code style
 8→
 9→By default, the `intellij_idea` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `ktlint_official` or `android`.
10→
11→```ini
12→[*.{kt,kts}]
13→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
14→```
15→
16→!!! note
17→    The default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` version of ktlint.
18→
19→## Disabled rules
20→
21→!!! note
22→    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
23→
24→Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
25→
26→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
27→```editorconfig
28→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
29→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
30→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
