The file /project/CHANGELOG.md has been updated.

When you're done with your current set of changes to this file, you should call the read_lints tool with the specific file path and fix any newly introduced errors.

1→In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 2→
 3→```ini
 4→[*.{kt,kts}]
 5→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 6→```
 7→
 8→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 9→
10→!!! note
11→    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
12→
13→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
14→
15→* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Be aware that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
16→
17→* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
18→

1→Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
 2→
 3→!!! danger
 4→
 5→    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
 6→
 7→## Code style
 8→
 9→By default, the `intellij_idea` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `ktlint_official` or `android`.
10→
11→```ini
12→[*.{kt,kts}]
13→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
14→```
15→
16→!!! note
17→    The default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` version of ktlint.
18→
19→## Disabled rules
20→
21→!!! note
22→    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
23→
24→Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
25→
26→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
27→```editorconfig
28→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
29→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
30→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)

16→

20→     */

23→    /**
24→     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
25→     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
26→     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
27→     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
28→     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When

30→     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
31→     * are still not fixed.

33→     */

35→}

CHANGELOG.md:13:* Change default code style to `ktlint_official`. Previously the default code style was `intellij_idea` (formerly known as `official`). The new `ktlint_official` code style adheres to the best parts of the Kotlin Coding Conventions and Android Kotlin styleguide. However, it is no longer restricted to produce code compatible with the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter which lacks fixes for bugs that still are not resolved after multiple years.
--
CHANGELOG.md:184:A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
--
CHANGELOG.md:186:This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
--
CHANGELOG.md:190:The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
--
CHANGELOG.md:385:* Add new code style `ktlint_offical`. The code style is work in progress and should be considered a preview. It is intended to become the default code style in the next release. Please try it out and give your feedback. See [code styles](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/code-styles/) for more information. The following rules have been added to the `ktlint_official` code style (the rules can also be run for other code styles when enabled explicitly):
--
CHANGELOG.md:457:* For the new code style `ktlint_official`, do not allow wildcard imports `java.util` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic` by default. 1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→### Added
 8→
 9→* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
10→
11→### Changed
12→
13→* Change default code style to `ktlint_official`. Previously the default code style was `intellij_idea` (formerly known as `official`). The new `ktlint_official` code style adheres to the best parts of the Kotlin Coding Conventions and Android Kotlin styleguide. However, it is no longer restricted to produce code compatible with the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter which lacks fixes for bugs that still are not resolved after multiple years.
14→
15→### Removed
16→
17→### Fixed
18→
19→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
20→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))

1→The `ktlint_official` code style is introduced and is the default code style starting from ktlint `1.0`. This code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 2→
 3→!!! note
 4→    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
 5→
 6→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
 7→
 8→* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. In versions prior to ktlint `1.0`, this was the default code style.
 9→
10→* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
11→

1→Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
 2→
 3→!!! danger
 4→
 5→    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
 6→
 7→## Code style
 8→
 9→By default, the `ktlint_official` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `intellij_idea` or `android_studio`.
10→
11→```ini
12→[*.{kt,kts}]
13→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
14→```
15→
16→!!! note
17→    Starting from ktlint `1.0`, the default code style is `ktlint_official`. In earlier versions, the default was `intellij_idea`.
18→

10→public enum class CodeStyleValue {

14→     */
15→    android_studio,
16→
17→    /**
18→     * Code formatting based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). This
19→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in IntelliJ IDEA.
20→     */
21→    intellij_idea,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
25→     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
26→     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
27→     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
28→     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
29→     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
30→     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
31→     * are still not fixed.
32→     * Starting from ktlint 1.0, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
33→     */
34→    ktlint_official,
35→}
